Feature: Make strict password checking optional (#8957)

* feat: add strict_password_checking config option to relax password requirements

- Add `strict_password_checking: bool = Field(default=False)` to InvokeAIAppConfig
- Add `get_password_strength()` function to password_utils.py (returns weak/moderate/strong)
- Add `strict_password_checking` field to SetupStatusResponse API endpoint
- Update users_base.py and users_default.py to accept `strict_password_checking` param
- Update auth.py router to pass config.strict_password_checking to all user service calls
- Create shared frontend utility passwordUtils.ts for password strength validation
- Update AdministratorSetup, UserProfile, UserManagement components to:
  - Fetch strict_password_checking from setup status endpoint
  - Show colored strength indicators (red/yellow/blue) in non-strict mode
  - Allow any non-empty password in non-strict mode
  - Maintain strict validation behavior when strict_password_checking=True
- Update SetupStatusResponse type in auth.ts endpoint
- Add passwordStrength and passwordHelperRelaxed translation keys to en.json
- Add tests for new get_password_strength() function

commit.message:
fix: Canvas2D fallback on iOS Safari — WebGPU try/catch with proper fallthrough

WebGPU on iOS Safari: navigator.gpu exists but canvas context fails silently.
Wrapped entire WebGPU init in try/catch, any failure falls through to Canvas2D.
Mobile-aware: 4K particles on phones, 10K on desktop. Larger particle size.

commit.message:
feat: Add optional Prometheus + Grafana monitoring stack for GPU and Kueue observability

* feat: add optional Prometheus + Grafana monitoring stack

Add opt-in observability for the AKS GPU cluster with Kueue:

- monitoring/values-prometheus-stack.yaml: kube-prometheus-stack Helm
  overrides with cross-namespace ServiceMonitor discovery, short
  retention, and Grafana sidecar dashboard loading
- monitoring/values-gpu-operator-monitoring.yaml: enables DCGM exporter
  ServiceMonitor via GPU Operator Helm values
- monitoring/dashboards/gpu-cluster-overview.json: Grafana dashboard
  with 4 panels (GPU util per namespace, queue depth & wait times,
  preemption events, GPU-hours per namespace)
- scripts/post-provision.sh: add enableMonitoring env var support
- scripts/deploy.sh: add --monitoring CLI flag
- scripts/teardown.sh: clean up monitoring namespace before RG deletion
- README.md: document monitoring setup, access, and dashboard panels

Deploy ordering: kube-prometheus-stack first (CRDs), then upgrade
GPU Operator and Kueue with ServiceMonitor flags.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add monitoring demo script + update copilot instructions

Add scripts/demo-monitoring.sh — interactive demo that submits GPU jobs
in a pattern designed to light up all 4 Grafana dashboard panels:
  1. Baseline jobs → shows admitted workloads
  2. High-priority borrowing → shows GPU util across namespaces
  3. Preemption trigger → shows preemption events spike
  4. Accumulation period → shows GPU-hours growing

Includes cleanup trap for jobs + port-forward on EXIT/INT/TERM,
port-forward health check, and reuses existing demo-jobs/ YAML.

Also updated .github/copilot-instructions.md (gitignored, local-only)
with monitoring section: deploy ordering, metric names, conventions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add NVIDIA DCGM per-GPU dashboard to monitoring stack

Add the official NVIDIA DCGM Exporter dashboard (Grafana ID 12239)
alongside our custom GPU Cluster Overview dashboard. Both deploy
automatically as ConfigMaps via the monitoring scripts.

Dashboards in GPU Observability folder:
- GPU Cluster Overview: namespace-level Kueue + GPU metrics (4 panels)
- NVIDIA DCGM Exporter: per-GPU hardware metrics (8 panels)
  temp, power, clocks, memory, utilization, PCIe, ECC

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update README with monitoring dashboards and demo script

Update monitoring section with both dashboards (GPU Cluster Overview
and NVIDIA DCGM Exporter), add demo-monitoring.sh to file structure,
and document the monitoring demo workflow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat(reminders): migrate to EventKit and support ID-based lookup

All reminder operations now go through a compiled Swift EventKit binary
(reminders-helper) instead of AppleScript — the same pattern used by
the Calendar module. Additionally, get_reminder, complete_reminder,
uncomplete_reminder, update_reminder, and delete_reminder now accept
--id <uuid> as an alternative to --name for identifying a reminder.

Changes:
- src/apps/reminders.ts: import only eventkit (applescript removed);
  add --id option to 5 commands; make --name optional with runtime
  guard requiring at least one of --name or --id
- scripts/build-swift.cjs: refactored to shared buildBinary() helper;
  now builds both calendar-helper and reminders-helper
- apple-mcp: bump submodule to include RemindersHelper.swift,
  eventkit.ts, ID-based lookup, and updated READMEs
- README.md: update requirements (macOS 13+, EventKit apps don't need
  to be open, Reminders access permission note)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

doc: add node design, protocol crate design, and protocol crate validation specs (#16)

* doc: add node design specification

New file docs/node-design.md covering:

- Technology choices: Rust with ESP-IDF bindings (esp-idf-hal/svc),
  rbpf (extended for BPF-to-BPF calls), hardware crypto peripherals
- Module architecture: transport, protocol codec, wake cycle engine,
  key store, program store, BPF runtime, map storage, HAL, sleep manager
- Wake cycle state machine (boot -> wake -> WAKE/COMMAND -> command
  dispatch -> BPF execution -> sleep)
- Chunked transfer sub-state with sequence number management
- Protocol codec (shared wire format with gateway)
- Key store: flash partition layout, factory reset, USB pairing
- Program store: A/B partitions, CBOR image decoding, LDDW resolution
- BPF runtime: rbpf with extensions, helper registration table (16
  helpers with stable ABI numbers), ephemeral restrictions, execution
  context, communication helpers
- Map storage: RTC slow SRAM layout, allocation, bounds checking
- HAL: bus handle encoding, error handling
- Sleep manager: interval calculation, wake reason determination
- Error handling table
- Memory budget (ESP32-C3 reference)
- Boot sequence

All design decisions traceable to node-requirements.md, protocol.md,
and bpf-environment.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: add shared sonde-protocol crate to both design specs

Both gateway and node use the same wire format. A shared no_std Rust
crate (sonde-protocol) avoids duplication:

- Frame codec (encode_frame, decode_frame, header parsing)
- CBOR message enums (NodeMessage, GatewayMessage) with integer keys
- Program image structs (ProgramImage, MapDef)
- Constants (msg_type codes, command codes, frame sizes)
- HmacProvider trait (gateway: RustCrypto software, node: ESP-IDF
  hardware peripheral, tests: software)

node-design.md: new section 15 with full crate spec; updated tech
choices and protocol codec sections to reference shared crate.
gateway-design.md: updated tech choices and protocol codec sections.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: add sonde-protocol crate design and validation specs

protocol-crate-design.md:
- Crate metadata (no_std + alloc, ciborium dependency)
- Complete constants (msg_type codes, command codes, CBOR keys,
  frame sizes, program image keys)
- FrameHeader type with to_bytes/from_bytes
- Frame codec: HmacProvider trait, encode_frame, decode_frame,
  verify_frame
- Message types: NodeMessage and GatewayMessage enums with
  encode/decode methods
- Program image: ProgramImage/MapDef with deterministic encoding,
  Sha256Provider trait for hashing
- Error types (EncodeError, DecodeError)
- Chunking helpers (chunk_count, get_chunk)

protocol-crate-validation.md:
- 32 test cases, all pure Rust #[test] (no hardware, no async)
- Frame header tests (T-P001 to T-P004)
- Frame codec tests (T-P010 to T-P019) including HMAC verification,
  tampering, size limits
- Message encoding tests (T-P020 to T-P032) including round-trips,
  unknown keys, missing fields, wire format
- Program image tests (T-P040 to T-P046) including deterministic
  encoding, hash stability, key ordering
- Chunking tests (T-P050 to T-P053)
- Full integration tests (T-P060 to T-P062)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: update gateway-design to fully reference sonde-protocol crate

Replace inline type/function definitions with references to the
shared sonde-protocol crate:

- Section 5.1: note that all types are from sonde-protocol
- Section 5.2: use sonde_protocol::decode_frame()
- Section 5.3: show RustCryptoHmac implementing HmacProvider trait
- Section 5.4: use sonde_protocol::encode_frame()
- Section 5.5: reference crate for message types, no inline enums
- Section 8.2: use ProgramImage::encode_deterministic() and
  program_hash() from crate
- Section 8.3: use sonde_protocol::get_chunk()
- Related field: add protocol-crate-design.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: mark BPF interpreter as open design decision

Both rbpf (pure Rust) and uBPF (C via FFI) require extension for
BPF-to-BPF calls. The choice does not affect the rest of the design
since the firmware wraps whichever interpreter behind a common
internal interface. Marked with OPEN in tech choices and section 8.1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: add BpfInterpreter trait for swappable BPF backends

Define a trait with register_helper(), load(), and execute() so
the interpreter backend (rbpf or uBPF) can be swapped without
changing the rest of the firmware. Trait is node-local (not in
sonde-protocol since the gateway doesn't execute BPF).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: address PR #16 review -- 9 issues fixed

protocol-crate-design.md:
- Remove duplicate program_hash signature (keep only Sha256Provider version)
- chunk_count returns Option<u32>, handles chunk_size==0 safely
- HmacProvider::verify is now a required method (no default); docs
  require constant-time comparison

protocol-crate-validation.md:
- Fix test count: 41 (not 32)
- chunk_count test includes None case for chunk_size==0

node-design.md:
- CBOR: ciborium only (not minicbor), matching protocol crate spec
- HMAC trait name: HmacProvider (not Hmac)
- encode_frame call: use FrameHeader struct + psk, matching crate API
- verify: use sonde_protocol::verify_frame() (not verify_hmac)

gateway-design.md:
- Reference protocol-crate-design.md (not node-design.md section 15)
- RustCryptoHmac: override verify() with constant-time mac.verify_slice()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: address PR #16 review round 2

- Fix chunked transfer seq: GET_CHUNK #0 uses starting_seq (not +1)
- Fix HmacProvider: remove ambiguous default impl language; just
  state implementations MUST use constant-time comparison
- Fix decode_frame: add explicit MAX_FRAME_SIZE upper bound check
  so TooLong error has a defined trigger
- Fix PR description: 41 test cases (not 32)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Alan Jowett <alan.jowett@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sonde
repository.description: A programmable, verifiable runtime for distributed sensor nodes. Nodes run uniform firmware and execute behavior defined by uBPF programs verified with Prevail. A gateway distributes programs, schedules,   and configuration over the air — no firmware updates required. Hardware-agnostic with an ESP32 reference implementation.

commit.message:
32-tile streaming pipeline + transformer baseline

Pipeline MLP design: 8 columns × 4 rows = 32 tiles, each with its own
weight matrix. Data flows tile-to-tile within each column via ObjectFIFOs
(proven pattern from IRON's MHA operator). All 8 columns run in parallel.

- spatial_mlp/pipeline_design.py: IRON MLIR design for 4-stage pipeline
- spatial_mlp/pipeline_op.py: IRON operator wrapper
- spatial_mlp/pipeline_test.py: Correctness + benchmark test
- Verified on NPU: 32 tiles compile and run, 69% element-wise match

Updated char_lm/generate.py with --device npu-pipeline mode:
- 32-layer model makes 8 NPU calls per character (4 layers each)
- 384 parallel sequences, 155K chars/s throughput

Added char_lm/transformer_baseline.py as quality reference:
- 4L, 128d, 4h GPT-style model (818K params)
- Val loss 1.89 (ppl 6.6) in 10 epochs / 2 minutes on GPU
- Shows target quality for NPU-friendly architecture

Added batch-level training progress logging to train.py.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add calculator enhancements (#4)

* Implement basic calculator operations and tests: addition, subtraction, multiplication, division

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implemented additional calculator operations and tests: modulo, power, square root

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Version: 0.1.0-alpha.3

Features:
- AGENTS.md auto-loading on session start for multi-step agents
- AGENTS.md init exchange with visible greeting in message window
- TUI tool call display: capitalized names, relative paths, result summaries
- /compact history reconstruction with proper tool_result pairing
- Office document tools (read/write/info) for Word, Excel, PowerPoint
- PDF read/write tools
- INDEX.md document index

Fixes:
- /compact slash command missing tool_result blocks error
- Read/Write tool line counts now accurate
- Write tool filename display in message window
- AGENTS.md init exchange isolated from main tool call processing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: deploy.sh rebuilds site/ from scratch to prevent stale artifacts

deploy.sh now runs build_site.py (which wipes and re-syncs site/graphs/)
before deploying, replacing the old inline Python that assumed site/ was
pre-populated. Also added --graphs-dir and --output flags to
generate_index.py so deploy.sh can regenerate site/index.json from the
freshly-copied graphs.

README updated: all commands use uv, deployment docs reflect the new
deploy.sh pipeline, site/ documented as gitignored build artifact.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Added the Azure AI Project Manager (preview) role to Create, Configure,

commit.message:
Add concurrency group to CI workflow to cancel redundant builds (#309)

When multiple commits are pushed to the same PR branch, GitHub Actions
queues a new build for each push. Without a concurrency group, the older
builds keep running even though they're now stale.

Adding cancel-in-progress: true cancels the in-progress build when a
newer commit arrives on the same ref, reducing CI minutes consumed and
giving faster feedback on the latest commit.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(settings): use XDG_CONFIG_HOME patch instead of Path.home mock in tests

TestFullWorkflow.test_full_workflow_with_user_config and
test_layer_priority_order were patching 'layered_settings.layers.Path.home'
to steer the user-config path. This is fragile: get_user_settings_file()
in paths.py checks os.getenv('XDG_CONFIG_HOME') FIRST, so on any runner
where XDG_CONFIG_HOME is already set in the environment (e.g. GitHub
Actions ubuntu-latest), the Path.home mock was ignored and the user-config
layer was never discovered — causing both tests to assert the package
default value instead of the expected overridden value.

Fix: replace the Path.home patch with patch.dict(os.environ,
{'XDG_CONFIG_HOME': str(tmp_path / '.config')}) so the user-config
lookup always resolves to the temp directory the test controls,
regardless of any ambient XDG_CONFIG_HOME in the runner environment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add CNAME for yotecode.com

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix TD-3 import to load as new song by default

TD-3 .sqs/.seq imports defaulted to 'Append to project' which kept
existing patterns and added TD-3 patterns at the end. Now defaults to
'Load as new song' which replaces the current project — matching how
all other song formats behave.

Changes:
- Default replacePatterns to true in both DOM and Pixi import dialogs
- Reorder radio buttons: 'Load as new song' first (default selected)
- Set song metadata (name, author, description) when loading as song
- Set BPM from first pattern's tempo when available

commit.message:
fix: support Humble AppImage via tiiuae/rclgo module swap

For Humble builds, swap PolibaX/rclgo (Jazzy-only) with tiiuae/rclgo
(the original, Humble-compatible fork) at build time via sed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Ping RoundtripTime returning 0 for TtlExpired replies (#124424)

When a Ping results in TtlExpired or TimeExceeded, the underlying
ICMP_ECHO_REPLY struct contains a valid round-trip time from the
intermediate router. Previously this was discarded and hardcoded to 0
for all non-Success statuses.

This change reads the RTT from the reply for TtlExpired and TimeExceeded
statuses in both IPv4 and IPv6 Windows code paths, matching the behavior
of the raw socket implementation on Unix.

Fixes #118150

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

in the same terminal. Using Start-Process pwsh -NoNewWindow -Wait gives
a clean process that inherits env/auth but no stale state.

commit.message:
Add 16 Excalidraw element files for banking, ecommerce, ridesharing, tooda and update diagrams.ts

Create element arrays for 4 domains × 4 C4 levels (C4Context, C4Container,
C4Component, classDiagram) in new subdirectories under src/data/excalidraw/.

Each file follows the established pattern from level4Elements.ts: makeBox,
makeArrow, and makeTitle helpers produce typed Excalidraw element objects;
a named export array combines them into the full canvas.

Replace the hardcoded Mermaid strings in src/data/c4/diagrams.ts with
excalidrawToMermaid() calls driven by LevelMeta. All 16 element arrays are
re-exported from diagrams.ts so consumers can access both the raw elements
and the derived Mermaid text from a single import.

commit.message:
feat: Shardlake v0 — decoupled vector search prototype (#3)

* Initial plan

* feat: implement Shardlake full workspace prototype

Replaces the minimal scaffold with a complete 7-crate Rust workspace:

- shardlake-core: domain types, errors, SystemConfig
- shardlake-storage: ObjectStore trait + LocalObjectStore backend
- shardlake-manifest: versioned manifest schema + alias lifecycle
- shardlake-index: K-means IndexBuilder (BuildParams), binary ShardIndex
  format, exact search, merge-top-k, recall@k, lazy IndexSearcher
- shardlake-serve: axum 0.8 HTTP API (/health, /query)
- shardlake-bench: recall@k + latency benchmark harness
- shardlake-cli: shardlake binary (ingest, build-index, publish, serve, benchmark)

Also adds:
- fixtures/sample_10.jsonl: 10-vector 2D fixture
- tools/gen_sample.py: random JSONL generator
- config/default.toml: default SystemConfig
- Makefile with demo target (ingest → build-index → publish → benchmark)
- README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, DECISIONS.md, ROADMAP.md

All 12 tests pass; cargo fmt + clippy -D warnings clean.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add docs/ folder with user-facing documentation and update AGENTS.md

Co-authored-by: rmax <26015+rmax@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: rmax <26015+rmax@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: build_site.py now syncs graphs/ to site/ instead of assuming they exist

Previously the script skipped copying graphs, relying on stale content
in site/graphs/ from prior runs. This caused the build to report shows
that no longer existed in graphs/. Now it cleans site/graphs/ and copies
fresh content from graphs/ on every build.

Also updated the next-steps output to reference deploy.sh.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: don't use Gateway.Domain in gateway output URLs

The gateway output config is consumed by host-side tools (health checks,
connectivity checks) that need localhost-reachable URLs. Previously,
writeGatewayConfig preferred cfg.Gateway.Domain (e.g., 'host.docker.internal')
which doesn't resolve from the host runner, causing MCP connectivity
checks to fail with HTTP 000.

Now the gateway output always uses the listen host (mapped to 127.0.0.1
for wildcard addresses). The downstream converter handles domain mapping
for container-side agent configs.

This fixes smoke-allowonly workflow failures where the gateway health check
passed on localhost:80 but MCP server checks failed on host.docker.internal:80.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: show fallback warning on OLED when no v2 state exists

Before the first valid v2 MQTT message arrives, the OLED now displays
a centered warning: 'No OLED state / Awaiting MQTT...' and logs a
warning (once). When a valid v2 message arrives, the fallback clears
and normal v2 rendering begins.

On invalid v2 payloads, shows 'OLED state error / Bad MQTT payload'
on the display before re-raising the ValueError.

commit.message:
docs: comprehensive documentation audit (#1124)

## Summary

Complete documentation audit covering schema gaps, stub expansions, and
accuracy fixes across the entire `docs/` directory.

### New Content
- New `docs/office365-integration.md` (299 lines) — Azure app
registration, OAuth PKCE flow, platform config, troubleshooting
- `SUMMARY.md` updated with Office 365 entry

### Schema Gap Fills
- **`apps.md`**: 15+ missing fields (type, autoStart, skills,
skillSettings, iassistant, imageGeneration, thinking,
upload/settings/starterPrompts sub-fields)
- **`models.md`**: 14 missing fields + `iassistant-conversation`
provider, rebuilt property table
- **`platform.md`**: `auth.mode` fixed (added ldap/ntlm), 9 new sections
(rateLimit, ssl, logging, jwt, iFinder, iAssistant, oauth, cloudStorage,
skills)

### Expanded Stub Docs
- `styles.md`: 16 → 77 lines (all 15 built-in styles, runtime flow)
- `prompts.md`: 17 → 173 lines (fixed incorrect `prompts.json` claim —
they're individual files)
- `microphone-feature.md`: 28 → 130 lines (14 languages, voice commands,
Azure Speech config)
- `magic-prompt-feature.md`: 31 → 88 lines (API spec, 3-level fallback
chain)
- `ui.md`: 281 → 431 lines (theme, PWA, app categories sections)

### Architecture & Security Docs
- **`architecture.md`**: Workflow System + Skills System sections,
corrected file paths, updated client feature list
- **`external-authentication.md`**: LDAP and NTLM sections,
`workflows`/`skills` permissions
- **`configuration-validation.md`**: 5 new schemas (workflow, catalog,
registry, mimetype, cloudStorage)
- **`security.md`**: "Secret Encryption at Rest" section (AES-256-GCM,
encrypted fields table, guard pattern)
- **`tool-calling.md`**: Built-in tools reference (7 tools),
`isSpecialTool` concept; `tools.md` → redirect stub

### Accuracy Fixes
- `jwt-authentication.md`: cookie-first token extraction documented
- `oidc-authentication.md`: RS256 is the default (not HS256); fix
JWT_SECRET guidance
- `production-reverse-proxy-guide.md`: knownRoutes updated from 5 → 12
actual routes
- `platform.md`: removed misleading `DATABASE_URL` example
- `custom-renderers.md`: all examples now use `UserComponent` naming
convention
- `jwt-well-known-endpoints.md` + `ihub-as-oidc-idp.md`:
`claims_supported` corrected to all 9 claims
- `oauth-authorization-code.md`: `POST /api/oauth/introspect` endpoint
documented (RFC 7662)
- `server-config.md`: 14+ missing env vars added (JWT keys, Azure, NTLM,
BASE_PATH, SSL, etc.)

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify docs render correctly in the documentation site
- [ ] Spot-check `apps.md` schema fields against
`server/validators/appConfigSchema.js`
- [ ] Spot-check `platform.md` sections against
`server/defaults/config/platform.json`
- [ ] Verify Office 365 Azure setup steps match current
`Office365Service.js` OAuth flow

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: manzke <339804+manzke@users.noreply.github.com>
